data.title: Privia Health's ACOs report 32% rise in Medicare savings
data.description:
• Privia Health Group (NASDAQ:PRVA) said that its Accountable Care Organizations ((ACOs)) achieved shared savings of $233.1M through the Medicare shared savings program for the 2024 performance year, up 32% compared to 2023.
  • In light of these results, Privia's management has raised its Adjusted EBITDA guidance for the full year of 2025, projecting it to be between $113M and $116M.
  • PRVA stock is up about 6%.

data.title: Nobel Laureate Lars Peter Hansen: Technology is more promising than global political consensus
data.description:
Technological advancement in the climate space represents a potentially more promising path forward than waiting for global political cooperation, according to Lars Peter Hansen, professor at the University of Chicago.

During a CNBC interview, the Nobel laureate said that current methods for valuing climate policy impacts need significant improvement, particularly when dealing with long-term projections fraught with uncertainty.

Uncertainty must be central to global climate policy discussions, as “we need to make policy in the face of uncertainty, and we need to be open about it. We need to give up on the false pretense of knowledge.”

He said that global leadership on climate issues appears to be moving in the wrong direction, with populist politics fostering short-term economic thinking rather than long-term planning.

“It makes it very difficult to establish credible long term policy gains and goals,” Hansen noted, adding that political coordination on a global scale will be “incredibly challenging.”

Current political environments, particularly in the U.S., have created significant setbacks for climate initiatives with massive pullbacks in government-funded research, he said, and expressed serious concern about this trend, stating, “It’s not only money going into it, it’s also intellectual capital going into it. Smart people are not going to see great prospects here in terms of their short-term futures… and they’ll be looking to other ventures.”

Lastly, Hansen advocated for a “long game” strategy focused on investing in carbon capture technologies and potentially revolutionary energy sources like nuclear fusion, while acknowledging these solutions may be decades away.

“I think it’s important that even though these are highly uncertain prospects that we consider investments across the board, [that] we get some successes along some of the demand,” he concluded, while emphasizing that short-term emission reductions remain necessary to “buy us more time.”

Green Energy ETFs: (NASDAQ:ICLN), (TAN), (FAN), (GRID), (QCLN), (NYSEARCA:ERTH)

AI and robotics ETFs: (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (GINN), (ROBT), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ), (CHAT)

data.title: Gryphon Digital confirms shareholders' nod for American Bitcoin deal, 5-for-1 reverse stock split
data.description:
Gryphon Digital Mining (NASDAQ:GRYP) confirmed on Friday stockholders' approval for the proposed merger with American Bitcoin, a mining firm backed by President Donald Trump's sons.

The transaction was approved at the Special Meeting of Stockholders held on Wednesday, August 27.

The strategic stock-for-stock transaction was announced in May.

GRYP shares were -7.85% to $1.59 during Friday morning trade.

Gryphon is now set to implement a reverse stock split at a ratio of 5-for-1, effective 5:00 PM ET on September 2.

The share consolidation aims to satisfy Nasdaq's listing requirements for minimum bid price.

New CUSIP number will be 02462A104.

Gryphon Digital's outstanding shares are expected to reduce from ~82.8M to ~16.6M.

data.title: ADM to close Illinois soy protein plant, streamline production network
data.description:
Archer Daniels Midland (NYSE:ADM) -0.3% in Friday's trading after saying it plans to cease operations at its plant in Bushnell, Illinois, as part of its plan to streamline its global soy protein production network.

ADM (NYSE:ADM) also said it expects operating profit at its recently recommissioned plant in Decatur, Illinois, to improve over time as it achieves manufacturing efficiencies and builds back the specialty ingredients business.

The company has been cutting jobs and downsizing some operations since announcing in February that it planned to cut costs by $500M-$700M over 3-5 years.

ADM (ADM) said in April it would permanently close a soybean processing plant in South Carolina to cut costs; it was the first U.S. soybean processing plant to close following a multi-year, industry-wide expansion.

The company said the changes will allow it to consolidate its soy protein operations into a more efficient global network while maintaining the ability to support growing global demand.

data.title: Zscaler Q4 earnings preview: What to expect
data.description:
When Zscaler (NASDAQ:ZS) reports fourth quarter earnings on Tuesday, Wall Street expects the cloud security company to post EPS of $0.80, implying a 9.1% decline, while revenue is estimated to increase 19.3% to $707.14 million during the quarter.

Analysts believe Zscaler can continue to expand both operating and free cash flow margin on the heels of accelerating revenue growth.

Zscaler’s CFO Remo Canessa guided Q4 revenue in the range of $705 million to $707 million, "reflecting a year-over-year growth of approximately 19%." Gross margin is expected to be approximately 80%, with operating profit between $152 million and $154 million, and earnings per share in the range of $0.79 to $0.80.

Cantor reiterated its Overweight rating on Zscaler with a price target of $340.

“We see sustained demand for enterprise SASE solutions and a long growth runway powered by Zero Trust Everywhere platform consolidation, emerging products, and expansion among large customers,” said Cantor analyst Jonathan Ruykhaver.

As per the Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings, ZS has a Hold rating with a score of 3.34 out of 5. The company received an A- for profitability and B+ for momentum, revisions and growth, but a D- grade in valuation dragged down the rating.

However, Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street analysts are bullish about the company and rated it as Buy.

Truist Securities also reiterated its Buy rating with a price target of $350.

“We also expect Zero Trust for Branch, one of the key pillars of Zero Trust Everywhere, to be a significant growth contributor in FY26, with 59% of the customers who bought the product in FQ3 being new logos (57% in FQ2). In addition, Data Security continues to be a growth vector for ZS, now over $350M of ARR (as of 2FQ25), as customers look to protect their data estate with the proliferation of LLMs,” said Truist Securities analyst Junaid Siddiqui.

Shares in the company were up 56.50% since the start of the year, outperforming the broader S&P Index, which gained nearly 10.5% during the same period.

Over the last two years, ZS has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 100% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen 38 upward revisions, compared to two downward moves, while revenue estimates have seen 20 upward revisions against 16 downward revisions.

data.title: Senate Democrats warn that Fannie, Freddie IPO plan may boost mortgage rates
data.description:
Three Senate Democrats are calling on the White House to halt efforts to sell shares of mortgage giants Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) and Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) until they can assess how the initial public offering will affect mortgage rates, according to a media report.

Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) stock fell 1.5% and Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) stock weakened 2.1% in late morning trading on Friday.

In a letter sent Friday morning to Federal Housing Finance Agency Director William Pulte, Senators Elizabeth Warren (D-MA), Cory Booker (D-NJ), and Chuck Schumer (D-NY) also demanded that he focus on housing affordability instead of spending time on such things as the Federal Reserve's building renovation and mortgage allegations against Lisa Cook, the Wall Street Journal reported.

Analysts have said that privatizing Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) and Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) could increase mortgage rates if investors aren't assured that the government would back them in the case of a severe financial downturn. The government took control of the two companies in 2008 when a spate of defaults put the two on the verge of collapse. 

Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) and Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC) are an important source of liquidity for the housing market. They don't originate mortgages. Rather, they buy mortgages, bundle them, and repackage them into mortgage-backed securities that they sell to investors. Their backing by the U.S. government provides a sense of assurance to investors. Together, Fannie and Freddie support ~70% of the mortgage market, according to the National Association of Realtors.

If investors have less confidence in the two companies' MBSs, they would demand a higher interest rate, which would mean mortgage rates would have to climb with that.

Treasury Scott Bessent has said the Trump administration is moving carefully on selling shares of Fannie Mae (OTCQB:FNMA) and Freddie Mac (FMMC) to avert rising mortgage rates.

As of Aug. 28, the 30-year fixed rate mortgage rate averaged 6.56%, down from 6.58% in the prior week, but above 6.35% in the same period a year ago, according to Freddie Mac (OTCQB:FMCC). In May 2020, during the pandemic when the federal funds rate was near zero, the average 30-year fixed rate mortgage was about 3.2%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Ulta's conservative comp sales guidance spooks investors, but positives remain intact - analysts
data.description:
Ulta’s (NASDAQ:ULTA) second quarter results gave investors plenty to cheer for. Profit and sales were up, and the company’s gross profit margin expanded 90 basis points despite higher costs, all of which beat Wall Street’s expectations.

But after ratcheting higher in Thursday’s late trading, shares are in the red as the company’s conservative guidance, including expectations for comparable sales to slow, weighs on Ulta (NASDAQ:ULTA) stock.

Ulta’s (NASDAQ:ULTA) comparable store sales for Q2 increased by 6.7%, the highest for eight quarters and more than double expectations with equally encouraging gains in transactions and average ticket.

But looking ahead towards the second half of the year, Ulta (ULTA) estimates FY25 comparable sales will increase by 2.5% to 3.5%, an improvement from the original estimate of flat to up 1.5%, but still a marked deceleration from Q2. While Wall Street analysts believe this is the company’s efforts to remain conservative in a still uncertain economic environment, it does show a slowdown in traffic.

“The company does have a 100-basis point harder comparison, but even with that, comps should be in the 3% to 4% range,” said DA Davidson analyst Michael Baker.

Evercore ISI’s Michael Binetti gives an even starker assessment of the comp estimates.

“With ticket consistently growing 2% to 3%, we think Ulta’s 2H guidance assumes either traffic will abruptly turn negative YOY or that it’s hard-fought return to share gains will reverse again – both are scenarios that we think will prove overly conservative.”

On the earnings call, CEO Kecia Steelman acknowledged the deceleration in comp sales, pointing to the “higher comps in the back half of the year,” and the “uncertainty that is still out there that remains with the consumer and macroeconomic conditions.”

“We’re being prudent in our guidance and how we’re looking at the business,” Steelman added.

Additionally, there is “significant noise” starting the second half of the year, Binetti says, including the company’s exit from Target (TGT), expansion in Mexico, and Space NK acquisition.

But despite the noise and conservative outlook, Ulta (ULTA) is still positioned to continue building momentum in the beauty category thanks to its increased focus on newness and exclusives, including the upcoming launch of Fenty Skin Body. The company is also taking on bolder marketing initiatives such as promotional events at Lollapalooza and Coachella and sponsoring Beyonce’s summer tour.

“Combine that with some new initiatives put in place by the new CEO as well as some better industry trends, and Ulta looks like a turnaround story with still room to go,” Davidson’s Baker adds.

So, while Ulta (ULTA) has pulled back from its 17-month high as investors weigh the FY25 outlook and lock in profits, analysts remain broadly bullish, with most reiterating Buy/Overweight ratings and lifting price targets as high as $680—implying a 28% upside from Thursday’s close.

Shares last traded nearly 6% lower, eroding some of the company's year-over-year 45% gain, but still soundly outperforming a 16% advance in the S&P 500.

data.title: Globus Medical wins favorable jury verdict in patent dispute with Life Spine
data.description:
A Delaware jury has sided with Globus Medical (NYSE:GMED), issuing a verdict in favor of the orthopedic product maker in a patent infringement case against its rival Life Spine over the latter’s Prolift family of implants.

In a statement late Thursday, Globus (NYSE:GMED), headquartered in Audubon, Pennsylvania, noted that the jury also awarded it lost profits and royalty damages after determining that Prolift implants violated its patent claims.

At issue are expandable spinal fusion implants designed to improve outcomes in spinal surgery.

The verdict comes at a time when Globus (GBUS) awaits the outcome of another patent dispute with Alphatec, Inc., over its expandable implant and retractor technology.

“We are pleased with the jury’s decision, which affirms the strength of our intellectual property and the groundbreaking innovation behind our expandable implant technologies,” CEO Keith Pfeil remarked.

data.title: Nevada casinos see gaming win improve on strong hold rates for slots and table games
data.description:
Gaming win on the Las Vegas Strip increased on a year-over-year comparison in July in a report that could boost sentiment in the closely watched sector.

Statewide gaming win was up 4.0% year-over-year in June to $1.31 billion. The Las Vegas Strip saw a solid increase in gaming win of 5.6% to $709.3 million. Downtown Las Vegas casinos saw gaming win rise 3.6% during the month to $71.8 million. Meanwhile, Reno casinos saw gaming win increase 9.9% during the month to $69.3 million.

Across the state, gaming win was up 2.7% for the slot machine business on a win percentage of 7.4%. Table/counter/card game gaming win shot up 7.1% on a strong win percentage of 15.8%. Baccarat revenue was up 16.9% during the month against a soft comparable from a year ago. Craps and roulette gaming win were up at a double-digit pace. Gaming win at sportsbooks rose 9% year-over-year for the traditionally slow month of July.

Las Vegas tourism trends have been soft this year, with visitor volume, hotel occupancy rates, average daily room rates, and airport passenger traffic all down compared to a year ago. High resort fees and rising service prices are considered a major factor, as well as soft international tourism trends, especially from Mexico and Canada, per analysts sizing up recent data.

Sector watch: Caesars Entertainment (NASDAQ:CZR), MGM Resorts (NYSE:MGM), Boyd Gaming (BYD), Wynn Resorts (WYNN), Full House Resorts (FLL), Bally's Corporation (BALY), Sportradar (SRAD), Genius Sports (GENI), and Red Rock Resorts (RRR).

data.title: Nasdaq slides 1% as chip stocks weigh; PCE inflation data keeps Sept rate cut in play
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday fell, weighed down by a slide in semiconductor stocks. Market participants also received a key in-line inflation reading that did little to change expectations of a Federal Reserve interest rate cut in September. 

The benchmark S&P 500 gauge (SP500) was last -0.8% in morning trade, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -0.5%. 

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slid -1.2%. Marvell Technology (MRVL) slumped -17.3% and was the top percentage loser on the tech-heavy index after the chipmaker's quarterly report disappointed investors. Other chip stocks also came under pressure. 

Meanwhile, Nvidia (NVDA), the world's largest chipmaker, declined -3.5%. A report said Chinese tech giant Alibaba (BABA) had developed a new artificial intelligence (AI) chip to help fill in the absence left by Nvidia (NVDA) in the Asian market. 

Also weighing on markets was Dell Technologies (DELL). The stock was -8.8% amid concerns over the PC and electronic maker's quarterly AI server margins. 

A busy economic calendar was also the focus on Friday, with the spotlight on the July core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index. Widely regarded as the Fed's preferred inflation gauge, the core PCE deflator rose +0.3 M/M, flat from June and matching the consensus estimate. On a Y/Y basis, it rose +2.9%, also in line with consensus but ticking up from June's +2.8% reading. 

"The headline PCE deflator rose 0.2% in the month taking the headline rate to 2.6%, and core PCE rose 0.3% to register a core rate of inflation of 2.9%. For core PCE, that is the quickest rate of year-over-year inflation since February," Wells Fargo's Tim Quinlan said. 

"Our forecast looks for some modest upward pressure on core inflation from goods prices and a bit less pressure from core services. On a nominal basis, two out of the three largest increases in spending in July were in services categories: financial services and housing. A potential challenge for the Fed as it tries to steer inflation lower is that service prices do not continue their trend descent that is showing signs of stalling in recent months," Quinlan added.  

The largely tame inflationary signal keeps the Fed on track for an interest rate cut in September, according to the CME FedWatch tool. 

"Overall, there is a reasonable case for dismissing some of the higher headline inflation numbers. But regardless there is still some elevation in inflation, one has to be nervous about transitory theories, and the real economy holding up OK," Jason Furman, former deputy director of the U.S. National Economic Council, said on X (formerly Twitter).

"On balance, I would do what Powell has signaled--which is one cut in September and then wait and see how everything is developing rather than locking in an expectation of more rate cuts," Furman added.

Speaking of the Fed, an emergency court hearing over Fed Governor Lisa Cook's lawsuit challenging President Trump’s attempt to remove her from the board is set to play out on Friday.  

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields were mixed. Longer-term maturities gained, while shorter-term instruments slipped, keeping the yield curve steep. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was last up 2 basis points to 4.23%, while the more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was down marginally to 3.63%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.
